title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Aluminum prices fell to 3,468.50 USD/T on April 2, 2026, down 1.57% from the previous day, though up 5.83% over the past month and 41.22% year-over-year. title: World's Top Aluminium Smelter Cuts Output Due to Shipping Chaos
summary: The world's largest single-site aluminium smelter in Bahrain, Aluminium Bahrain (Alba), cut its output by about 20% due to shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This shutdown affects three reduction lines, representing roughly 2.2% of global aluminium production and aims to preserve raw material inventory. The disruption could tighten global supply chains and pressure aluminium prices higher.

title: Aluminum Rally Builds as War Risks Threaten Global Supply Chains
summary: Aluminum prices are rising amid geopolitical tensions, Middle East disruptions, constrained global production, and China's capacity limits at 45.5 million tons. High energy costs have led to production cuts, such as Norse Hydro and Qatar Aluminium reducing output by 40% from 648,000 tons capacity, with Europe seeing 2.2 million tons offline. Global inventories are below 5-year averages, driving market volatility.

title: Aluminium Above $3,500, LNG Near $20, and Wheat Planting Hits ...
summary: Aluminium prices are climbing sharply due to direct disruptions to smelting capacity in the UAE and Bahrain, removing over 1 million tons of expected production. Facilities in the Gulf have sustained damage amid Strait of Hormuz issues, impacting exports through normal routes. These shocks are rippling through metals markets under geopolitical and cost pressures.

title: Panama moves to unlock copper from First Quantum mine
summary: Panama's Commerce and Industries Minister may approve by Tuesday the exports of already mined ore from Cobre Panama’s stockpiles. This move aims to unlock copper production from First Quantum's operations amid ongoing discussions. It follows a revised plan for the vast copper mine complex that lowers near-term volumes while prioritizing long-term efficiency.

title: Rio Tinto's Nuton technology produces first copper
summary: Rio Tinto has produced the first copper cathode using its proprietary Nuton bioleaching technology at the Gunnison Copper’s Johnson Camp mine, relying on microorganisms for extraction. This technology offers up to 80% less water usage and 60% lower carbon emissions compared to traditional methods, with a mine-to-metal carbon footprint of 0.82 kg CO₂-e per kg copper—the lowest in the U.S. Nuton extends mine life by processing waste ores and supports the domestic U.S. copper supply chain.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel futures are trading around $17,100, falling from March highs near $17,770, amid ongoing structural tightness and cautious investor sentiment. Supply constraints persist due to Indonesia's proposed nickel export tax and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. Nickel traded flat at 17,100 USD/T on April 6, 2026, down 1.89% over the past month but up 18.42% year-over-year.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today - Nickel Price Analysis Today Apr 6, 2026
summary: Nickel prices remained flat at $17,081.3/Ton globally and ¥117,559/Ton in China, within a tight trading range. The market is supported by Indonesia’s reduced ore production quotas easing oversupply fears, recovering Chinese stainless steel and EV demand, and declining LME inventories. Upside is capped by tiered Indonesian royalty rates at higher prices.

title: Golden Flowers and Green Nickel: Albania is Pioneering New Era of Sustainable and Eco-Friendly Mining Innovation
summary: Albanian Minerals and Metalplant are collaborating to produce 100% Green, Eco-Friendly Nickel, revolutionizing metal sourcing with ecological responsibility. This innovation, called NegativeNickel, sequesters 200 tonnes of CO2 per tonne of nickel produced using green magnesium silicates, achieving over 70% net efficiency. The partnership introduces NeNegativeNickel, HyperNickel, and HyperWeathering, promoting industry-nature harmony.

title: New Technology Set to Revolutionize Nickel Pollution Cleanup
summary: Albanian Minerals leverages olivine, a natural green mineral from its vast reserves, to neutralize acidic nickel-contaminated environments and immobilize toxic nickel from water and soil. Olivine weathering also captures atmospheric CO2, addressing both pollution and climate change simultaneously. This scalable solution restores ecosystems, supports agriculture, and promotes a cleaner future.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries reported zero lost-time injuries and 17.7 million safe man-hours in its quarterly activities report, emphasizing workforce safety. The company acquired a 10% stake in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt and secured an offtake agreement for nickel cathode products. It received awards including the Indonesia Leadership Award 2025 and Excellence in Sustainability Leadership from CNBC Indonesia.
